track.title: Money Market Funds
track.description: Beginner lessons for understanding Kenyan MMFs, yield language, fees, tax, withdrawals, and provider checks.

course.title: Money Market Funds: Core route
course.description: Learn how MMFs work before treating them as savings or emergency routes.

lessons.title: What is a Money Market Fund?
lessons.summary: An MMF pools money and invests mainly in short-term instruments.
lessons.introduction: A Money Market Fund pools money from many investors and invests mainly in short-term instruments. For a beginner, the important questions are provider status, withdrawal time, fees, and whether the money is needed soon.
sections: MMFs are often used for short-term saving because they usually aim for liquidity, but access rules still vary by provider.
The fund manager, trustee, custodian, fees, and fact sheet are part of the route, not background details.
The value can grow through income from the underlying instruments, but quoted figures can change.

scenario.title: First salary saver
scenario.text: A first-jobber has KES 5,000 for short-term saving. They compare withdrawal timing and fund documents before choosing whether to use an MMF.

lessons.mistake: Treating an MMF like a bank account without checking redemption timing and fees.
lessons.action: Find the fund manager, trustee or custodian, fees, and withdrawal rule before sending money.
lessons.takeaway: An MMF can be simple to use, but it still deserves verification.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify approved schemes and market players using CMA public sources.

lessons.title: How MMFs make money
lessons.summary: MMFs earn from instruments held by the fund, not from a fixed promise to every user.
lessons.introduction: An MMF earns income from the assets it holds, such as short-term deposits or government securities, depending on the fund mandate. The investor sees a quoted yield or daily accrual, but this can change.
sections: The fund pools money so the manager can buy instruments according to the mandate.
Fees and tax treatment affect what reaches the investor.
A fund's past quoted yield does not remove market, liquidity, provider, or operational risk.

scenario.title: Headline yield comparison
scenario.text: Two funds show different yields. The user checks fees, fund documents, withdrawal timing, and provider status before deciding what the number means.

lessons.mistake: Assuming the highest displayed yield tells the whole story.
lessons.action: Compare yield basis, fees, tax, withdrawal time, and provider status side by side.
lessons.takeaway: Understand the engine behind the number before trusting the number.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Use CMA and provider documents to verify fund structure and current terms.

lessons.title: Yield, fees, withholding tax, and withdrawal time
lessons.summary: A useful MMF comparison includes net yield, fees, tax questions, and redemption timing.
lessons.introduction: MMF decisions often start with yield, but yield alone is not enough. Fees, withholding tax questions, cut-off times, and withdrawal processing can change the real experience.
sections: Ask whether the quoted yield is gross or net of fees where the provider gives that information.
Tax treatment can change and depends on current law and product structure, so users should verify with KRA, provider documents, or a tax professional.
Withdrawal timing matters for emergency money. Same-day, next-day, and longer timelines are very different in practice.

scenario.title: Emergency withdrawal
scenario.text: A user needs KES 6,000 tomorrow. A fund with a higher quote but slower withdrawal may not fit that need.

lessons.mistake: Ignoring cut-off times and discovering withdrawal timing only during an emergency.
lessons.action: Write the withdrawal process in plain language before relying on an MMF.
lessons.takeaway: The useful number is the one you understand after fees, tax questions, and timing.
lessons.source_note: Source note: KRA pages are tax education references only; PesaRoute does not give tax advice.

lessons.title: Why highest yield is not always best
lessons.summary: A higher displayed yield can come with trade-offs you should understand.
lessons.introduction: The highest displayed yield can attract attention, but a beginner should ask what sits behind it. Provider status, fees, liquidity, disclosure quality, and suitability for the goal matter.
sections: A yield is one comparison point, not a full decision.
Withdrawal time can matter more than a small yield difference if the money is for emergencies.
A provider that explains risks, fees, and documents clearly may be easier to evaluate.

scenario.title: KES 20,000 choice
scenario.text: A user compares three MMFs. One has the highest quote, but another has clearer fees and faster withdrawal. They write why liquidity matters for their goal.

lessons.mistake: Choosing by a leaderboard screenshot without verifying the route.
lessons.action: Compare three funds using yield, fees, withdrawal time, source documents, and provider status.
lessons.takeaway: A good MMF decision is about fit, not chasing a single number.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify current license and scheme details with CMA and the provider.

lessons.title: MMF simulator practice
lessons.summary: Use the simulator to see how principal, time, and rate assumptions affect an estimate.
lessons.introduction: The MMF simulator is a learning tool. It helps you see how assumptions change an estimate, but it is not a statement of what a fund will pay.
sections: Try changing the rate to see how sensitive the estimate is.
Try a shorter time period if the money may be needed soon.
Write the assumptions in your journal so you remember what the estimate depended on.

scenario.title: Three-month estimate
scenario.text: A user tests KES 10,000 over three months and notices the result changes when rate assumptions move.

lessons.mistake: Treating a simulator output as a promise or instruction.
lessons.action: Run one MMF simulation and write one assumption that could be wrong.
lessons.takeaway: Simulation teaches sensitivity; it does not remove uncertainty.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify actual fund terms directly with providers.

lessons.title: Journal prompt: Why am I choosing an MMF?
lessons.summary: Record the reason, timeline, amount mode, and reason you might wait.
lessons.introduction: A private journal entry helps separate a calm decision from a rushed one. For MMFs, write why liquidity, fees, provider status, and withdrawal timing fit your goal.
sections: Use exact, rounded, range, or hidden amount mode depending on your comfort.
Name the goal and when the money may be needed.
Write one reason this route may not fit, even if you still proceed externally.

scenario.title: Hidden amount note
scenario.text: A user writes 'KES 5k-20k emergency buffer' instead of an exact amount and records the withdrawal rule they checked.

lessons.mistake: Saving only the product name without the reason and exit rule.
lessons.action: Create a journal entry with goal, amount mode, reason, and risk note.
lessons.takeaway: A decision note helps future you understand present you.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Keep private notes local-only or synced depending on your privacy choice.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should be checked besides MMF yield?
quiz_questions.explanation: Yield is only one input; fees and access matter.

quiz_questions.prompt: Who should users verify tax treatment with when needed?
options: KRA, provider documents, or a tax professional
quiz_questions.correct_answer: KRA, provider documents, or a tax professional
quiz_questions.explanation: PesaRoute does not give tax advice.

quiz_questions.prompt: What is an MMF simulator output?

flashcards.back: A pooled fund that mainly invests in short-term instruments.
flashcards.example: A user may compare MMFs for short-term savings.

flashcards.back: It depends on fund assets, market conditions, fees, and other factors.
flashcards.example: A quote this month can differ later.

flashcards.back: They affect when withdrawal requests are processed.
flashcards.example: Emergency money may need faster access.

flashcards.back: Provider status, fees, liquidity, disclosures, and goal fit.
flashcards.example: A lower quote with clearer terms may be easier to evaluate.

flashcards.front: What should an MMF journal note include?
flashcards.back: Goal, amount mode, reason, withdrawal rule, and risk note.
flashcards.example: KES 5k-20k emergency buffer, withdrawal expected next day.

resources.title: MMF checklist
resources.body: A beginner checklist for comparing Kenyan Money Market Funds by provider status, fees, yield basis, tax questions, withdrawal timing, and source documents.
items: Verify fund manager and approved scheme information.
Write withdrawal timing and cut-off rules.
Use KRA/provider/professional sources for tax questions.
